<!-- Generated by /write-a-prd — product requirements with user stories and EARS acceptance criteria -->

# Lecturer Guide — Interactive Cheat Sheet

## Problem Statement

The lecturer is preparing to deliver a 2-day cybersecurity course (12 teaching hours) to 25–30 employees of a water utility company. The existing lecturer's guide (`Ръководство_за_лектора.md`) contains comprehensive content but is a flat 850-line Markdown file that is hard to navigate during live delivery. The lecturer needs a tool that lets them quickly drill down to any topic, subtopic, or talking point with a single click — not scroll through a wall of text under pressure.

## Solution

A locally-running React + Vite + Tailwind CSS application that presents the lecturer's guide as an interactive, hierarchical reference tool. Content is organized as a tree of collapsible sections — modules at the top, expanding into sections, subsections, and individual talking points at arbitrary depth. A persistent sidebar provides module-level navigation, and a "next topic" button enables linear flow through the entire course without losing the ability to jump around.

This is a personal tool for the lecturer — not a student-facing presentation. The priority is fast orientation, dense information access, and visual clarity.

## User Stories

### 1. Application Startup

**As** the lecturer, **I want** to start the app with a single terminal command and see it in my browser, **so that** I can quickly launch it before or during the course.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

1. WHEN the lecturer runs `npm run dev` in the `lecturer-guide/` directory, THE system SHALL start a local development server and display the app URL in the terminal
2. WHEN the lecturer opens the displayed URL in a browser, THE system SHALL render the full application with the sidebar and the first module's content visible
3. WHEN the app starts, THE system SHALL display the Introduction section ("Въведение и организационни бележки") as the default view

### 2. Sidebar Navigation

**As** the lecturer, **I want** a persistent sidebar listing all course modules, **so that** I can jump to any module instantly.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

1. THE system SHALL display a left sidebar visible at all times, listing all navigable sections in course order: Introduction, Module 1 through Module 6, Day 1 Summary, Day 2 Review
2. WHEN the lecturer clicks a module in the sidebar, THE system SHALL scroll/switch the main content area to display that module's content
3. THE system SHALL visually highlight the currently active module in the sidebar (distinct background or border color)
4. WHEN a module is active in the main content area, THE system SHALL reflect that state in the sidebar highlighting

### 3. Collapsible Sections

**As** the lecturer, **I want** content organized in collapsible sections that I can expand/collapse with a click, **so that** I see only the detail level I need at any moment.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

1. WHEN the lecturer clicks a section header, THE system SHALL toggle that section between expanded and collapsed states with a smooth animation (200-300ms CSS transition)
2. THE system SHALL support nesting collapsible sections to at least 4 levels deep (module → section → subsection → detail)
3. WHEN a section is collapsed, THE system SHALL show only the section title and a visual indicator (chevron/arrow) pointing right
4. WHEN a section is expanded, THE system SHALL show the section title with a visual indicator pointing down, followed by the section's content
5. WHEN a parent section is collapsed, THE system SHALL hide all nested children regardless of their individual expand/collapse state
6. WHEN a parent section is re-expanded, THE system SHALL restore nested children to their previous expand/collapse states

### 4. Content Type Visual Markers

**As** the lecturer, **I want** different types of content (talking points, examples, transitions, time markers) to be visually distinct, **so that** I can instantly identify what I'm looking at.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

1. THE system SHALL render "Какво да кажеш" (talking points) content with a distinct visual style — left border or background color indicating "key points"
2. THE system SHALL render "Примери" (examples) content with a different distinct style — visually differentiated from talking points (e.g., different accent color, italic text, or icon)
3. THE system SHALL render "ПРЕХОД →" (transition) notes with a distinct style — visually prominent as a "next step" indicator (e.g., arrow icon, contrasting color)
4. THE system SHALL render time markers (⏱) with a distinct style — clock icon or badge showing the time window and day
5. THE system SHALL render Q&A markers with a distinct style indicating a break/interaction point
6. WHEN content contains bold terms with definitions (e.g., "**Threat (Заплаха):** description"), THE system SHALL render the term visually emphasized and the definition clearly associated with it

### 5. Next/Previous Topic Navigation

**As** the lecturer, **I want** "next topic" and "previous topic" buttons, **so that** I can follow the course linearly without manually finding the next section.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

1. THE system SHALL display a "next topic" button in a fixed position (bottom-right or bottom of the content area)
2. THE system SHALL display a "previous topic" button alongside the next button
3. WHEN the lecturer clicks "next topic", THE system SHALL navigate to the next section in the linear course order, expand it, and scroll it into view
4. WHEN the lecturer clicks "previous topic", THE system SHALL navigate to the previous section in the linear course order, expand it, and scroll it into view
5. THE system SHALL define the linear order as: Introduction → Module 1 sections (1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4) → Day 1 Q&A → Module 2 sections → ... → Day 1 Summary → Day 2 Review → Module 5 remaining sections → Module 6 sections → Final summary
6. WHEN the lecturer is at the first section, THE system SHALL disable or hide the "previous topic" button
7. WHEN the lecturer is at the last section, THE system SHALL disable or hide the "next topic" button
8. WHEN the lecturer navigates via next/previous, THE system SHALL update the sidebar highlighting to reflect the new active module

### 6. Module Content Structure

**As** the lecturer, **I want** each module's content to follow a consistent structure matching the existing guide, **so that** the information feels familiar and predictable.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

1. THE system SHALL organize each module with a top-level header showing the module title and duration (e.g., "Модул 1: Въведение в киберсигурността (50 мин)")
2. THE system SHALL display a time marker at the module level showing which day and time window it belongs to
3. THE system SHALL organize each module's subsections (e.g., 1.1, 1.2) as collapsible sections within the module
4. WITHIN each subsection, THE system SHALL present content in this order: talking points ("Какво да кажеш"), then examples ("Примери"), then transition note ("ПРЕХОД →")
5. THE system SHALL render bullet lists with proper indentation for nested items (e.g., attack types with sub-bullets for descriptions)

### 7. Data Architecture

**As** a maintainer (Claude Code), **I want** content stored in structured data files separate from UI components, **so that** content can be updated without touching component logic.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

1. THE system SHALL store course content in JavaScript/TypeScript data files, with one file per module (e.g., `module1.js`, `module2.js`)
2. EACH content file SHALL export a structured object with: module title, duration, time marker, and an array of sections
3. EACH section SHALL contain: title, content type markers (talking points, examples, transitions), and an optional array of nested subsections
4. THE system SHALL have a separate index file that defines the linear navigation order across all modules and special sections (intro, summaries)

### 8. V1 Content Migration

**As** the lecturer, **I want** all content from the existing `Ръководство_за_лектора.md` migrated into the new app, **so that** I have everything I need from day one.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

1. THE system SHALL include all content from the existing guide: Introduction, Modules 1–6, Day 1 Summary, Day 2 Review
2. THE system SHALL preserve all talking points, examples, transition notes, and time markers from the source document without omission
3. THE system SHALL preserve the bilingual convention — Bulgarian text with English technical terms in parentheses (e.g., "Заплаха (Threat)")
4. THE system SHALL preserve all real-world examples with their details (company names, years, figures)

## Glossary

- **Talking points (Ключови тези / Какво да кажеш):** The main content the lecturer should say — bullet points covering the topic
- **Examples (Примери):** Real-world cases and analogies to illustrate the talking points
- **Transition (ПРЕХОД →):** A scripted sentence bridging from the current topic to the next one
- **Time marker (⏱):** Indicates which day and time window a module/section belongs to
- **Q&A:** A 10-minute question-and-answer break after each module

## Implementation Decisions

- **Technology stack:** React + Vite + Tailwind CSS
- **Directory:** `lecturer-guide/` subdirectory within the existing `cybersecurity-course` repository
- **Component architecture:**
  - `App` — top-level layout with sidebar and main content area
  - `Sidebar` — module list with active state highlighting
  - `CollapsibleSection` — recursive component supporting arbitrary nesting depth with animated expand/collapse
  - Content marker components — visual styling for each content type (talking points, examples, transitions, time markers)
  - `NavigationControls` — fixed next/previous topic buttons
- **Data layer:** Structured JS objects in per-module data files, imported by the app. No runtime Markdown parsing — content is pre-structured at build time
- **Styling:** Tailwind utility classes. Color-coded content types. Dark or light theme — single theme, no toggle needed for v1
- **No routing library** — single-page app with scroll-based navigation and state for active module

## Testing Decisions

- Manual testing by running `npm run dev` and clicking through all modules
- Verify all 6 modules + intro + summaries render with complete content
- Verify next/previous navigation visits every section in correct order
- Verify collapsible sections animate and nest correctly
- No automated tests for v1 — the app is a personal tool with visual output; manual verification is sufficient

## Out of Scope

- Student-facing presentation mode
- Search/filter functionality
- Timer/countdown widget
- Dark/light theme toggle
- Mobile responsiveness (used on a laptop/desktop only)
- Automated tests
- Content expansion beyond the existing guide (will be done incrementally post-v1)
- Deployment or hosting — runs locally only

## Further Notes

- The linear navigation order must account for the course spanning 2 days, with Module 5 split across Day 1 (section 5.1) and Day 2 (sections 5.2–5.4), and interstitial sections (Day 1 Summary, Day 2 Review) in between
- Content will be expanded post-v1 by adding deeper nested sections within existing modules — the collapsible architecture must support this without code changes
- All future content updates will be done via Claude Code editing the data files
